howItWorks.intro: This door rough opening calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Rough Opening Width = Door Width + 2 inches (pre-hung)
Rough Opening Height = Door Height + 2.5 inches (pre-hung)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a rough opening?
faq.answer: The rough opening is the framed opening in the wall that receives the door (or window). It is always larger than the door itself to allow for the door frame, shims, and adjustment. Typically 2 inches wider and 2.5 inches taller than the door.

faq.question: What size header do I need above a door?
faq.answer: For interior non-load-bearing walls: a flat 2x4 is sufficient. For load-bearing walls: doubled 2x6 up to 4 ft, 2x8 up to 6 ft, 2x10 up to 8 ft opening. Always check local code for load-bearing walls.

faq.question: Is the Door Rough Opening Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What door width should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate door width value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Door Rough Opening Calculator?
sections.body: The Door Rough Opening Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your door width, door height, door type, you get instant results including rough opening width, rough opening height, header size needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting rough opening width right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your door width and need to find the right rough opening width. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
